title: ProjectEncyclopedia
description: Consolidated index of memory/, obsidian_vault/ and project root, organised into 12 fixed categories. Built by W0.5 / memory_bundle builder, consumed by agents at the start of each block.

version.description: Monotonic version of the encyclopedia snapshot.

generated_at.description: ISO8601 timestamp when the encyclopedia was generated.

bundle_mode.description: True when the builder ran in GitHub Actions bundle mode (vault+root only), False for full local mode.

priority_1_top.description: List of top-priority-1 HIGH-VALUE items featured in the landing of encyclopedia.html.

source_stats.description: High-level statistics about the source corpus.

memory_files.description: Number of files indexed under memory/.

vault_files.description: Number of files indexed under obsidian_vault/.

root_files.description: Number of files indexed at the project root level.

total_lines.description: Aggregate line count across all indexed files (optional).

total_items.description: Aggregate item count across all categories (alternative to total_lines).

total_mb.description: Aggregate size in megabytes across all indexed files.

categories.description: Fixed set of 13 category buckets. Every key must be one of the canonical category_ids (12 originales + modelos_activos nuevo tras ajuste de alcance S61).

description.description: Description of what belongs in this category.

icon.description: Optional emoji or icon for the category.

item.description: Either a regular file item (path, preview, size_bytes, lines) or a model card (script_path, metrics, source_memory, status). Only id, title, tags, priority are strictly required.

id.description: Relative path used as the unique identifier of the item.

title.description: Title extracted from the file (first heading or filename).

preview.description: First ~200 characters of the file content.

tags.description: Free-form tags attached to the item (e.g. #canonical, #S60, #tier1).

priority.description: Priority tier: 1 = HIGH-VALUE (top-20), 5 = lowest. Used to rank items per category.
